title: NPS (Net Promoter Score) Calculator
metaDescription: Calculate your Net Promoter Score from promoter, passive, and detractor response counts, instantly in your browser.
shortDescription: Calculate NPS from survey response counts across promoters, passives, and detractors.

guideSections.heading: Why Passives Are Excluded From the Score Itself
guideSections.body: NPS is calculated as the percentage of promoters minus the percentage of detractors — passives count toward the total respondent pool but don't appear in the subtraction. This is intentional: passives are considered neutral enough that they don't meaningfully predict referral behavior in either direction.

guideSections.heading: Why a Single NPS Number Hides as Much as It Reveals
guideSections.body: Two companies can both report an NPS of 40 with very different underlying distributions — one with mostly promoters and few detractors, another with a more polarized mix of strong promoters and strong detractors balancing out. Looking at the underlying percentages, not just the final score, shows which situation you're actually in.
